frameworks.name: random utility model
frameworks.one_line: cardinal neural observable drives stochastic choice probabilities.

frameworks.name: neuroeconomics
frameworks.one_line: linking neural activity during consumer-good valuation to choice.

core_question: Can a cardinal model relate neural observables to stochastic choice behavior with quantifiable predictive power?
key_assumptions: neural activity carries a cardinal (not just ordinal) value signal
stochasticity in choice is tied to the cardinality of the neural measurement
manipulations: subjects evaluated consumer goods while neural activity was measured
neural observable type compared for predictive power
key_findings: neural activity predicts subsequent choice behavior
degree of choice stochasticity tracks cardinality of the neural measurement
current measurement methods carry substantial measurement error
interpretations: cardinal neural observables can in principle support a random-utility account of choice
measurement error currently limits inferential and predictive performance of this approach
limitations_noted: significant measurement error in current neural methods
